ops.title: agent-browser 0.31.1 state-load mechanics (daemon-side path read, FIFO works, --state - never existed) and the abwc skill 0.7.1 recipes built on them
ops.body:
Empirically verified on agent-browser 0.31.1 (brew, vercel-labs/agent-browser — third-party, Node wrapper + Rust binary):

- `--state -` / stdin state NEVER existed in any version. `--state` was silently inert from the Rust rewrite until 0.26.0 (2026-04-16) wired the load; ≥0.26.0 opens the path, so `-` fails ENOENT.
- `state load <path>` resolves the path in the background daemon: regular files and named FIFOs work; `/dev/stdin` reads the daemon's closed stdin and fails. FIFO drained in one synchronous read (81KB > pipe buffer verified, no double-open). Exits 0 even on bad payload — failure is only the `✗ Invalid state file` stdout line.
- `cookies set --curl <path>` parses foreground-side: `/dev/stdin` works. Auto-detects bare cookie-string / JSON array / full curl dump; a `Cookie:`-prefixed line silently sets ZERO cookies. `cookiesync cookies --format header` emits the bare pairs (compatible). Header format carries no domain — one injection per host.
- bash `<(...)` broken: the Node wrapper spawns the binary with stdio 0-2 only.

[[cookiesync-architecture]] skill agent-browser-with-cookies 0.7.1 (cc-skills) teaches: local = private `mktemp -d` FIFO + `state load` (one Bash call required); Browserbase = `cookies set --curl /dev/stdin` post-open. cookiesync installs via `brew install --cask yasyf/tap/cookiesync` (uv `cookiesync-cli` is the dead Python era). A concurrent session was adding webstorage/localStorage origins to cookiesync (uncommitted jj WIP, post-0.9.0) — the skill's "restores localStorage" claim assumes it ships.
